astronaut.example: The astronaut floats in space because there is no gravity!
astronaut.fun_fact: Astronauts train for years before going to space.

spacesuit.example: The spacesuit protects astronauts from cold and radiation.
spacesuit.fun_fact: A spacesuit costs about 12 million dollars!

rocket.example: The rocket launches into space with fire and smoke!
rocket.fun_fact: Rockets travel at over 17,000 miles per hour.

spacecraft.example: The spacecraft carries astronauts to the space station.
spacecraft.fun_fact: Spacecraft have special heat shields for re-entry.

spacestation.example: Astronauts live and work on the space station.
spacestation.fun_fact: The ISS is as big as a football field!

mission.example: The Apollo mission took humans to the Moon.
mission.fun_fact: The first Moon landing was in 1969.

gravity.example: Gravity keeps us on the ground and the Moon in orbit.
gravity.fun_fact: There is less gravity on the Moon than on Earth.

orbit.example: The space station orbits Earth every 90 minutes!
orbit.fun_fact: Things in orbit move very fast to stay up.

telescope.example: The Hubble Telescope takes amazing pictures of space.
telescope.fun_fact: Telescopes can see billions of light-years away.

launch.example: The rocket launch was so loud and exciting!
launch.fun_fact: Rockets use special fuel to blast off.

lunar.example: The lunar module landed on the Moon's surface.

earthrise.example: Astronauts saw the beautiful Earthrise from the Moon.
earthrise.fun_fact: The first Earthrise photo was taken in 1968.

spacewalk.example: During a spacewalk, astronauts float outside the spacecraft.

satellite.example: Satellites help us watch TV and use GPS.
satellite.fun_fact: There are thousands of satellites orbiting Earth.

cosmonaut.example: Cosmonaut is the Russian word for astronaut.
cosmonaut.fun_fact: The first person in space was a cosmonaut named Yuri Gagarin.

spaceshuttle.example: The space shuttle could land like an airplane.

rover.example: The rover drives on Mars and takes pictures.
rover.fun_fact: The Mars rovers have lasted much longer than planned.

landing.example: The landing was soft and safe in the ocean.
landing.fun_fact: Some rockets now land standing up like in cartoons!

apollo.example: Apollo 11 was the first mission to land on the Moon.
apollo.fun_fact: Neil Armstrong was the first person on the Moon.

nasa.example: NASA is the American space agency.
